questionHtml:
In which set are the salts arranged in order of increasing pH?

A.  HCOONH4 < KBr < NH4Br < HCOOK

B.  KBr < NH4Br < HCOOK < HCOONH4

C.  NH4Br < HCOONH4 < KBr < HCOOK

D.  HCOOK < KBr < HCOONH4 < NH4Br

meta.title: In which set are the salts arranged in order of increasing pH? A. HCOONH4 < KBr < NH4Br < HCOOK B. KBr < NH4Br < HCOOK < HCOONH4 C. NH4Br < HCOONH4 < KBr < HCOOK D. HCOOK < KBr < H
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.1.12—The pH of a salt solution depends on the relative strengths of the parent acid and base. Construct equations for the hydrolysis of ions in a salt, and predict the effect of each ion on the pH of the salt solution.
